```{r, echo=FALSE, results="hide"}
repeat {
  w <- sample(seq(0.20,0.80,0.05),1); mu1 <- sample(seq(-2,2,0.5),1)
  mu2 <- sample(seq(3,8,0.5),1); v1 <- sample(seq(0.5,3,0.5),1); v2 <- sample(seq(0.5,4,0.5),1)
  mu <- w*mu1+(1-w)*mu2
  ans <- w*(v1+(mu1-mu)^2)+(1-w)*(v2+(mu2-mu)^2)
  vals <- round(c(ans,w*v1+(1-w)*v2,(v1+v2)/2,mu^2,ans+mu^2),3)
  if (length(unique(vals)) == 5L) break
}
opts <- sprintf("$%.3f$",vals)
```

Question
========
Uma mistura tem peso `r w`, médias $\mu_1=`r mu1`$, $\mu_2=`r mu2`$ e variâncias $\sigma_1^2=`r v1`$, $\sigma_2^2=`r v2`$. Qual é a variância da mistura?

Answerlist
----------
* `r opts[1]`
* `r opts[2]`
* `r opts[3]`
* `r opts[4]`
* `r opts[5]`

Solution
========
Com $\mu=w\mu_1+(1-w)\mu_2$, a variância total é
$$w[\sigma_1^2+(\mu_1-\mu)^2]+(1-w)[\sigma_2^2+(\mu_2-\mu)^2]
=`r sprintf("%.3f",ans)`.$$

Meta-information
================
exname: Cap1S9Q10_variancia_mistura
extype: schoice
exsolution: 10000
exshuffle: TRUE
